Home How it works Why Velvoix Pilot Contact

Velvoix

From signal to clear care action

Many care signals arrive without enough context. That leaves staff to decide under pressure what is urgent and what should happen first. Velvoix turns a signal directly into a clear care action with context, priority and traceable follow-up.

Explore a pilot View the product tour

Less doubt, clearer prioritisation and better reconstruction afterwards.

At a glance

From alert to clear follow-up

Velvoix helps teams see sooner what needs attention, which next step fits and who should take it.

01

What comes in

An alert is not yet a clear action

Many signals arrive without enough context to act well straight away.

02

What Velvoix adds

Context, priority and recommended action

Velvoix helps teams see sooner what is urgent and which next step fits the situation.

03

What that delivers

Calmer and clearer follow-up

Status, responsibility and next steps remain visible without creating more interpretation work.

The challenge

When an alert is not yet a clear action

Many systems show that something is happening, but not immediately what needs attention, how urgent it is and which action is required. That leaves teams interpreting under pressure.

Explainability

Why

Help button was triggered repeatedly within a short interval.

Based on triage rules and alert context.

Primary signal

Repeated help request

Core observation

Further follow-up will likely be needed.

Triage basis

Repeat trigger in a short interval and the context of this alert.

Practical follow-up

Recommended action

Go directly to the resident; check the situation.

Urgency

Direct

SLA status

SLA critical

Owner

Not yet assigned

How it works

From signal to a clear next action

Velvoix combines signals with context and prioritisation, so care teams can see sooner what needs attention and what should happen next.

See faster what needs attention
Make the recommended action clear immediately
Keep follow-up and reconstruction easy to review

From signal to action

Not just another alert, but a clear next step with visible status, responsibility and reconstruction.

Step 1

Signal received

A button call, voice input or other care signal enters in a controlled way.

Step 2

Context added

History, repetition and the current situation are weighed in straight away.

Step 3

Urgency becomes clear

Priority and recommended action become visible in clear care language.

Urgency Direct
Why Repeated within a short interval
Action Go directly to the resident and check the situation

Step 4

Follow-up stays visible

Status, responsibility and the next step remain visible together for the team.

Status Open
Responsibility Not yet assigned
Follow-up Clear to the team straight away

Step 5

Progress stays easy to follow

Status changes and reconstruction stay easy to follow.

Why Velvoix is different

Why Velvoix works differently in practice

Velvoix does not stop at the alert, but helps teams move toward clear action and traceable follow-up.

01

Not just another alert

Velvoix goes beyond simply flagging that something happened.

Less interpretation work, calmer follow-up.

02

Clearer priority

Teams see faster what needs attention and what should happen first.

Better prioritisation, clearer action.

03

Follow-up stays visible in one place

Status, responsibility and next steps stay visible together for the care team and for review.

Clearer follow-up, better reconstruction.

Product Tour

Short product views of clear follow-up

Focused examples show how Velvoix brings clarity, follow-up and control together.

Not marketing mockups, but a controlled view of the product screens that matter most in practice.

18 selected screens

Selected screen

Clear action with context and follow-up

One example of how the reason, priority and next action stay visible together, with less interpretation work.

Product Tour

1 / 18

Start

A calm entry point

The sign-in experience stays calm and familiar so teams can start the day without extra friction.

Pinch or scroll to zoom. Drag to move. Press 0 to reset.

Reliable in practice

Built for care situations where pressure and dependencies meet

Velvoix remains useful when speed, clarity and traceability matter. Even when not every part of the chain responds ideally, it remains clear what is happening and what the next step should be.

Clear status
Honest fallback
Traceable follow-up
Bounded access
Manageable behaviour
Usable under pressure

01

Remains usable during partial disruption

Follow-up remains usable when parts of the chain are temporarily limited because core status and the next step stay visible.

Reliable workflow when not every part responds ideally.

02

No false certainty

Limited context or availability stays visible instead of disappearing behind apparent certainty.

Honest fallback instead of silent degradation.

03

Clear status as the starting point

Status, explanation and follow-up remain readable together, so teams do not have to rely on scattered interpretations.

One clear starting point for action.

04

Bounded access

Access and actions remain bounded by organisation, context and role.

Calm control over who can see and do what.

05

Reconstructable and auditable

Follow-up, status changes and critical decisions remain traceable for evaluation and quality improvement.

Traceable follow-up instead of isolated snapshots.

06

Manageable in practice

Behaviour, follow-up and review remain manageable so teams can respond in a controlled way without adding extra noise.

Control as part of day-to-day practice.

That keeps Velvoix clear and usable, even when care practice is under pressure.

Pilot validation

What a first pilot can validate in practice

A pilot shows whether Velvoix helps your organisation prioritise sooner, follow up more clearly and reconstruct events more effectively afterwards.

Seeing faster what needs attention

Which signals lead to clearer follow-up sooner, and where additional context genuinely changes decisions.

Clearer follow-up and responsibility

How status, responsibility and escalation reduce interpretation noise in day-to-day work.

Better reconstruction for review

Which audit and evaluation questions become easier to answer with clearer status progression and reconstruction.

Practical outcomes depend on staffing, processes, equipment and existing systems. That is why a targeted pilot is more useful than a generic demo.

A first exploration focuses on

01

Seeing faster what needs attention

Test whether teams can prioritise faster and which situations require immediate action.

02

Clearer follow-up

Assess whether responsibility, status and the next step can be organised more clearly.

03

Better reconstruction

Make visible whether status progression and reconstruction are useful for evaluation and quality improvement.

04

Fit with practice

Explore how Velvoix fits teams, processes, devices and the existing environment.

Focus on clearer follow-up, workload and practical fit in day-to-day care.
